data.title: Invitation to presentation of Electrolux Group Q1 report
data.description: Electrolux Group is set to release their first quarter results for 2026 on April 24th, 2026, at around 07:00 CEST. The company will host a video webcast and telephone conference for media, investors, and analysts on the same day.

data.title: ASSA ABLOY acquires Rollerdoor Group in Portugal
data.description: In a recent development on April 9, 2026, ASSA ABLOY announced the acquisition of Rollerdoor Group, a renowned sectional door manufacturer situated in Portugal. The move signifies ASSA ABLOY's strategic efforts to enhance its presence in established markets. This acquisition underscores the company's commitment to growth and innovation, aligning with their vision for expansion.

data.title: Skanska builds new double track railway in Norway for NOK 1.2 billion, about SEK 1.1 billion
data.description: In Stockholm on April 9, 2026, Skanska announced a new agreement with Bane NOR to undertake the Stange-Otterstad rail infrastructure endeavor in Norway. The contract holds a value of NOK 1.2 billion, approximately SEK 1.1 billion. This substantial deal will be reflected in Skanska's Nordic order bookings for the upcoming quarter.

data.title: PPWR: Why Duni Group Phased Out PFAS Before the Requirements
data.description: In Malmö, Sweden on April 9, 2026, the EU's Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR) will enforce more stringent material guidelines, particularly targeting PFAS chemicals in food contact packaging starting August 12, 2026. Duni Group has opted to gradually implement the changes to comply with the new regulations.

data.title: Innovaccer and Infinia Technologies Form Strategic Alliance to Power the Future of AI-Driven Healthcare in the UAE
data.description: A partnership has been formed between Innovaccer Inc., a prominent healthcare AI company, and Infinia Technologies, a subsidiary of Sirius International Holding in Abu Dhabi. Through this strategic alliance, the two companies aim to boost healthcare innovation in the United Arab Emirates. The collaboration, backed by Abu Dhabi's International Holding Company, signifies a joint effort to advance a digital-focused healthcare environment.

data.title: Skanska builds data center in Georgia, USA, for USD 75M, about SEK 690M
data.description: Skanska, a construction company based in Stockholm, has recently secured a deal with a current client to construct a data center in the United States specifically in Georgia. The total value of the contract stands at $75 million, approximately equivalent to SEK 690 million. This significant project will be reflected in the first quarter US order bookings for 2026.
